source_pr.title: session,executor: run statement without transaction
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

Some users  prefer `set @@autocommit = 0` ... `set @@autocommit = 1` to `begin ... commit`
Currently TiDB has problems with such kind of usage:

```
set @@autocommit = 0;  // get tso here, transaction start. oh, my god! 
// 30 minutes later ...
// ...
select xxx    // transaction should have start here
insert into t values (3)
```

select 1 // This statement should not make the session enter a transaction, because it never need Txn(). 
'set xxx' is similar


### What is changed and how it works?

Don't initialize `Txn()` for those statements that don't need it.
Delay active pending transaction as much as possible.

- ActivePendingTxn is removed from `ExecStmt.Exec` in adaptor.go
- Call Txn() will active pending txn, so the process is delayed until Txn() is called
- If Txn() is not called, the statement will not set `mysql.ServerStatusInTrans`
- Txn() function signature is changed, I'll update it in a separate PR as that involves too many changes


### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test

Code changes

 - Has interface methods change

symptoms: mux sync.Mutex // fix data race in ddl test.
failure_signature: mux sync.Mutex // fix data race in ddl test.
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to concurrency races or unsynchronized shared state. Asynchronous state transitions need polling/Eventually-style checks. This PR stabilizes it by: session,executor: run statement without transaction.

evidence: evidence: mux sync.Mutex // fix data race in ddl test.
